title: Tamil Nadu may delay school reopening over heatwave fears; govt seeks IMD forecast before final call - The Times of India

full_text:
CHENNAI: The school education department has sought inputs from the
India Meteorological Department (IMD) on possible heatwave-like conditions in the first week of June. Upon receiving them, a discussion will be held with chief minister C Joseph Vijay to postpone the reopening date of schools, school education minister Rajmohan told mediapersons on Monday.
The minister's response comes in the wake of several representations from school associations and education activists seeking the postponement of reopening date from June 1 to 8.
"A decision on this will be made soon," the minister said, after handing over appointment orders to 6 teachers appointed through the Teachers Recruitment Board.
To a query on sensitising students to child helplines , the minister said that the newly launched textbooks for classes 1-3 had these numbers printed on the first few pages to raise awareness. He further said that debates and special classes and orientation sessions to inculcate scientific temper in children will be held this academic year.
On the long-pending demands of part-time and secondary grade teachers', who staged state-wide protests this year before the elections, and the unfilled vacancies to teacher posts in elementary and middle schools, the minister said that a special grievance meeting will be held to tabulate their concerns.
"We will assess what can be done in each case. Some of these demands have administrative concerns while some are legal. We will tabulate the concerns and work for teachers' welfare. This govt stands by students, teachers and parents, in that order," he added.
